messages.content:
6. What is the purpose of an operating system?

a) To perform mathematical calculations
b) To manage hardware resources
c) To create websites
d) To design graphics
b) To manage hardware resources

7. Which type of computer memory is non-volatile and retains data even when power is turned off?

a) RAM (Random Access Memory)
b) ROM (Read-Only Memory)
c) Cache Memory
d) Virtual Memory
b) ROM (Read-Only Memory)

8. Which of the following is a cloud storage service provided by Google?

a) OneDrive
b) iCloud
c) Dropbox
d) Google Drive
d) Google Drive

9. What is the function of a router in a computer network?

a) To connect multiple devices to the internet
b) To store and retrieve data
c) To manage computer peripherals
d) To play multimedia files
a) To connect multiple devices to the internet

10. Which type of computer network covers a larger geographical area, such as multiple buildings or cities?

a) LAN (Local Area Network)
b) WAN (Wide Area Network)
c) MAN (Metropolitan Area Network)
d) PAN (Personal Area Network)
b) WAN (Wide Area Network)

11. Which of the following is a common programming language used for mobile app development?

a) HTML
b) C#
c) Swift
d) SQL
c) Swift

12. Which type of software is designed to protect a computer system from viruses and other malicious programs?

a) Firewall
b) Antivirus
c) Backup software
d) Video editing software
b) Antivirus

13. What does the acronym “HTTP” stand for?

a) HyperText Transfer Protocol
b) Home Transfer Transmission Protocol
c) HyperText Transfer Processing
d) Home Typing Text Program
a) HyperText Transfer Protocol

14. Which of the following is an example of a database management system?

a) Microsoft Excel
b) Adobe Photoshop
c) Google Chrome
d) MySQL
d) MySQL

15. Which of the following is not a component of a computer network?

a) Router
b) Modem
c) Printer
d) CPU
d) CPU

16. What is the purpose of a firewall in computer security?

a) To prevent unauthorized access to a network
b) To improve computer performance
c) To create backups of data
d) To connect different networks together
a) To prevent unauthorized access to a network

17. What does the acronym “CAD” stand for in the context of computer-aided design?

a) Computer-Aided Drawing
b) Computer-Aided Design
c) Computer Algorithm Development
d) Computer Analysis and Design
b) Computer-Aided Design

18. Which of the following is an example of an input/output device?

a) Mouse
b) Hard drive
c) RAM
a) Mouse

19. Which coding language is primarily used for statistical analysis and data manipulation?

a) Java
b) Python
c) C++
d) Ruby
b) Python

20. Which of the following is an example of an open-source operating system?

a) macOS
b) Windows
c) Linux
d) iOS

20. What does the acronym “HTML” stand for in web development?

a) HyperText Modeling Language
b) HyperText Markup Log
c) Hypertext Markup Language
d) Home Tool Management Language
c) Hypertext Markup Language
1. True or False: Artificial Intelligence is a technology that enables computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence.
True
